diagnostics.purpose: Post-replacement amplitude-dependence check used by the generalized-play command-path fit.

exclusions.reason: Reference gain 0.9331 was not reproduced; the next two standardized runs agree near 0.9742.
Constant-play validation failed at the standard 1.5 deg triangle amplitude.
Constant-play validation failed at the standard 1.5 deg triangle amplitude.
Preconditioning did not rescue constant-play validation at the standard 1.5 deg triangle amplitude.
The 3.0 deg triangle fit passes internally but is inconsistent with the 1.5 deg hysteresis, proving amplitude dependence.
Collected after motor replacement but before the final mechanical alignment; superseded by post-alignment runs.
The 1.5 deg single-play gate fails; this run is retained only inside the validated multi-amplitude fit.
The 1.5 deg single-play gate fails; this run is retained only inside the validated multi-amplitude fit.
The 3.0 deg single-play fit passes only at its own amplitude; use the cross-amplitude command-path artifact.

notes: The post-alignment 1.5 deg and 3.0 deg traces require a common linear-plus-play command path.

limitations: Delay is phase-equivalent closed-loop response delay, not pure transport latency.
Effective backlash includes compliance and static-friction hysteresis.
Coulomb current is not joint torque and must not be converted without calibration.
Ground-contact load dependence was not measured.
measurement_domain: rigid-upper-body suspended robot at 50 Hz using encoder/current telemetry
purpose: Reproducible aggregation of validated suspended RND STEP actuator-identification runs.
